title: Aphanochilus rugulosus
intro: Aphanochilus rugulosus, also known as Kutki, is a plant species in the family Asparagaceae, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Kutki include its ability to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a valuable remedy for various gastrointestinal issues and autoimmune diseases. The bioactive constituents of Kutki include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Kutki is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, powder, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including jaundice, fever, and skin disorders.
